definition: The grammar by which a consumer pins a Module-as-a-Service artifact: a floating-tag → exact-semver → content-hash ladder, each rung resolving down to the immutable content-addressed URL (the npm tag → version → tarball shape). Floating and exact pins are revalidatable short-TTL redirects; the content-hash rung is terminal and never revalidates. WE supports pinning at any rung and mandates none.
usage: Part 2 of the MaaS Served-Artifact Versioning Protocol. Unit of version is dual — a human semver on the definition and the derived content-addressed id coexist (human contract vs. integrity contract).
